title: SELECT, Projection, Expressions, Types, and Result Contracts · Query Repair Bay
summary: An operations analyst needs a readable, reproducible ticket extract whose columns carry explicit meaning. Learners isolate plausible failures, compare competing hypotheses, and record cause-level repair evidence. Planned artifact: Repair a changed failure in a typed ticket projection query pack and publish the reproduction, evidence, repair, and regression record. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Compose SELECT and FROM clauses that project only required columns from a named relation.
Use relation and column aliases to make provenance and output contracts unambiguous.
Build arithmetic, text, date, and CASE expressions while tracing input and output meaning.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

starterFiles.sql:
-- sql-basics cumulative relational data portfolio.
-- Each increment declares grain, predicts cardinality or changed rows, runs in fresh browser SQLite,
-- reconciles results, tests changed data, and records PostgreSQL or standard-SQL boundaries.

SELECT sqlite_version() AS browser_sqlite_version;

steps.title: Inspect the failure record · Debug Select From
steps.instruction:
debug case — Return unresolved high-or-critical tickets plus unowned work from the seeded database. Expose ticket_id, a readable owner label, age bucket 2, and deterministic severity/opened ordering; then test a NULL owner and tied timestamp.

Inspect the Select From trace for this debug case. Which record connects a precondition, observed state change, and boundary result?

Evidence target: Inspect the failure record · Debug Select From.
steps.why: The decision supplies observable evidence for compose select and from clauses that project only required columns from a named relation. For Inspect the failure record · Debug Select From, keep Select From connected to executable relational evidence.

content.title: Evidence defense
content.text: Use the case, one assumption, one observable result, and one limitation. Do not copy the competency statement: Use duplicate-preserving and duplicate-eliminating results intentionally and explain their cost and semantics.
